verses.esv: An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
verses.kjv: THE burden of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
verses.bsb: This is the burden against Nineveh, the book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite:

verses.esv: The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD is avenging and wrathful; the LORD takes vengeance on his adversaries and keeps wrath for his enemies.
verses.kjv: God is jealous, and the Lord revengeth; the Lord revengeth, and is furious; the Lord will take vengeance on his adversaries, and he reserveth wrath for his enemies.
verses.bsb: The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD is avenging and full of wrath. The LORD takes vengeance on His foes and reserves wrath for His enemies.

verses.esv: The LORD is slow to anger and great in power, and the LORD will by no means clear the guilty. His way is in whirlwind and storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.
verses.kjv: The Lord is slow to anger, and great in power, and will not at all acquit the wicked: the Lord hath his way in the whirlwind and in the storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.
verses.bsb: The LORD is slow to anger and great in power; the LORD will by no means leave the guilty unpunished. His path is in the whirlwind and storm, and clouds are the dust beneath His feet.

verses.esv: He rebukes the sea and makes it dry; he dries up all the rivers; Bashan and Carmel wither; the bloom of Lebanon withers.
verses.kjv: He rebuketh the sea, and maketh it dry, and drieth up all the rivers: Bashan languisheth, and Carmel, and the flower of Lebanon languisheth.
verses.bsb: He rebukes the sea and dries it up; He makes all the rivers run dry. Bashan and Carmel wither, and the flower of Lebanon wilts.

verses.esv: The mountains quake before him; the hills melt; the earth heaves before him, the world and all who dwell in it.
verses.kjv: The mountains quake at him, and the hills melt, and the earth is burned at his presence, yea, the world, and all that dwell therein.
verses.bsb: The mountains quake before Him, and the hills melt away; the earth trembles at His presence— the world and all its dwellers.

verses.esv: Who can stand before his indignation? Who can endure the heat of his anger? His wrath is poured out like fire, and the rocks are broken into pieces by him.
verses.kjv: Who can stand before his indignation? and who can abide in the fierceness of his anger? his fury is poured out like fire, and the rocks are thrown down by him.
verses.bsb: Who can withstand His indignation? Who can endure His burning anger? His wrath is poured out like fire; even rocks are shattered before Him.

verses.esv: The LORD is good, a stronghold in the day of trouble; he knows those who take refuge in him.
verses.kjv: The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
verses.bsb: The LORD is good, a stronghold in the day of distress; He cares for those who trust in Him.

verses.esv: But with an overflowing flood he will make a complete end of the adversaries, and will pursue his enemies into darkness.
verses.kjv: But with an overrunning flood he will make an utter end of the place thereof, and darkness shall pursue his enemies.
verses.bsb: But with an overwhelming flood He will make an end of Nineveh and pursue His enemies into darkness.

verses.esv: What do you plot against the LORD? He will make a complete end; trouble will not rise up a second time.
verses.kjv: What do ye imagine against the Lord? he will make an utter end: affliction shall not rise up the second time.
verses.bsb: Whatever you plot against the LORD, He will bring to an end. Affliction will not rise up a second time.

verses.esv: For they are like entangled thorns, like drunkards as they drink; they are consumed like stubble fully dried.
verses.kjv: For while they be folden together as thorns, and while they are drunken as drunkards, they shall be devoured as stubble fully dry.
verses.bsb: For they will be entangled as with thorns and consumed like the drink of a drunkard— like stubble that is fully dry.

verses.esv: From you came one who plotted evil against the LORD, a worthless counselor.
verses.kjv: There is one come out of thee, that imagineth evil against the Lord, a wicked counseller.
verses.bsb: From you, O Nineveh, comes forth a plotter of evil against the LORD, a counselor of wickedness.

verses.esv: Thus says the LORD, “Though they are at full strength and many, they will be cut down and pass away. Though I have afflicted you, I will afflict you no more.
verses.kjv: Thus saith the Lord; Though they be quiet, and likewise many, yet thus shall they be cut down, when he shall pass through. Though I have afflicted thee, I will afflict thee no more.
verses.bsb: This is what the LORD says:  “Though they are allied and numerous, yet they will be cut down and pass away. Though I have afflicted you, O Judah, I will afflict you no longer.

verses.esv: And now I will break his yoke from off you and will burst your bonds apart.”
verses.kjv: For now will I break his yoke from off thee, and will burst thy bonds in sunder.
verses.bsb: For I will now break their yoke from your neck and tear away your shackles.”

verses.esv: The LORD has given commandment about you: “No more shall your name be perpetuated; from the house of your gods I will cut off the carved image and the metal image. I will make your grave, for you are vile.”
verses.kjv: And the Lord hath given a commandment concerning thee, that no more of thy name be sown: out of the house of thy gods will I cut off the graven image and the molten image: I will make thy grave; for thou art vile.
verses.bsb: The LORD has issued a command concerning you, O Nineveh:  “There will be no descendants to carry on your name. I will cut off the carved image and cast idol from the house of your gods; I will prepare your grave, for you are contemptible.”

verses.esv: Behold, upon the mountains, the feet of him who brings good news, who publishes peace! Keep your feasts, O Judah; fulfill your vows, for never again shall the worthless pass through you; he is utterly cut off.
verses.kjv: Behold upon the mountains the feet of him that bringeth good tidings, that publisheth peace! O Judah, keep thy solemn feasts, perform thy vows: for the wicked shall no more pass through thee; he is utterly cut off.
verses.bsb: Look to the mountains— the feet of one who brings good news, who proclaims peace! Celebrate your feasts, O Judah; fulfill your vows. For the wicked will never again march through you; they will be utterly cut off.

verses.esv: The scatterer has come up against you. Man the ramparts; watch the road; dress for battle; collect all your strength.
verses.kjv: HE that dasheth in pieces is come up before thy face: keep the munition, watch the way, make thy loins strong, fortify thy power mightily.
verses.bsb: One who scatters advances against you, O Nineveh. Guard the fortress! Watch the road! Brace yourselves! Summon all your strength!

verses.esv: For the LORD is restoring the majesty of Jacob as the majesty of Israel, for plunderers have plundered them and ruined their branches.
verses.kjv: For the Lord hath turned away the excellency of Jacob, as the excellency of Israel: for the emptiers have emptied them out, and marred their vine branches.
verses.bsb: For the LORD will restore the splendor of Jacob like the splendor of Israel, though destroyers have laid them waste and ruined the branches of their vine.

verses.esv: The shield of his mighty men is red; his soldiers are clothed in scarlet. The chariots come with flashing metal on the day he musters them; the cypress spears are brandished.
verses.kjv: The shield of his mighty men is made red, the valiant men are in scarlet: the chariots shall be with flaming torches in the day of his preparation, and the fir trees shall be terribly shaken.
verses.bsb: The shields of his mighty men are red; the valiant warriors are dressed in scarlet. The fittings of the chariots flash like fire on the day they are prepared, and the spears of cypress have been brandished.

verses.esv: The chariots race madly through the streets; they rush to and fro through the squares; they gleam like torches; they dart like lightning.
verses.kjv: The chariots shall rage in the streets, they shall justle one against another in the broad ways: they shall seem like torches, they shall run like the lightnings.
verses.bsb: The chariots dash through the streets; they rush around the plazas, appearing like torches, darting about like lightning.

verses.esv: He remembers his officers; they stumble as they go, they hasten to the wall; the siege tower is set up.
verses.kjv: He shall recount his worthies: they shall stumble in their walk; they shall make haste to the wall thereof, and the defence shall be prepared.
verses.bsb: He summons his nobles; they stumble as they advance. They race to its wall; the protective shield is set in place.

verses.esv: The river gates are opened; the palace melts away;
verses.kjv: The gates of the rivers shall be opened, and the palace shall be dissolved.
verses.bsb: The river gates are thrown open and the palace collapses.

verses.esv: its mistress is stripped; she is carried off, her slave girls lamenting, moaning like doves and beating their breasts.
verses.kjv: And Huzzab shall be led away captive, she shall be brought up, and her maids shall lead her as with the voice of doves, tabering upon their breasts.
verses.bsb: It is decreed that the city be exiled and carried away; her maidservants moan like doves, and beat upon their breasts.

verses.esv: Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. “Halt! Halt!” they cry, but none turns back.
verses.kjv: But Nineveh is of old like a pool of water: yet they shall flee away. Stand, stand, shall they cry; but none shall look back.
verses.bsb: Nineveh has been like a pool of water throughout her days, but now it is draining away. “Stop! Stop!” they cry, but no one turns back.

verses.esv: Plunder the silver, plunder the gold! There is no end of the treasure or of the wealth of all precious things.
verses.kjv: Take ye the spoil of silver, take the spoil of gold: for there is none end of the store and glory out of all the pleasant furniture.
verses.bsb: “Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold!” There is no end to the treasure, an abundance of every precious thing.

verses.esv: Desolate! Desolation and ruin! Hearts melt and knees tremble; anguish is in all loins; all faces grow pale!
verses.kjv: She is empty, and void, and waste: and the heart melteth, and the knees smite together, and much pain is in all loins, and the faces of them all gather blackness.
verses.bsb: She is emptied! Yes, she is desolate and laid waste! Hearts melt, knees knock, bodies tremble, and every face grows pale!

verses.esv: Where is the lions' den, the feeding place of the young lions, where the lion and lioness went, where his cubs were, with none to disturb?
verses.kjv: Where is the dwelling of the lions, and the feedingplace of the young lions, where the lion, even the old lion, walked, and the lion’s whelp, and none made them afraid?
verses.bsb: Where is the lions’ lair or the feeding ground of the young lions, where the lion and lioness prowled with their cubs, with nothing to frighten them away?

verses.esv: The lion tore enough for his cubs and strangled prey for his lionesses; he filled his caves with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
verses.kjv: The lion did tear in pieces enough for his whelps, and strangled for his lionesses, and filled his holes with prey, and his dens with ravin.
verses.bsb: The lion mauled enough for its cubs and strangled prey for the lioness. It filled its dens with the kill, and its lairs with mauled prey.

verses.esv: Behold, I am against you, declares the LORD of hosts, and I will burn your chariots in smoke, and the sword shall devour your young lions. I will cut off your prey from the earth, and the voice of your messengers shall no longer be heard.
verses.kjv: Behold, I am against thee, saith the Lord of hosts, and I will burn her chariots in the smoke, and the sword shall devour thy young lions: and I will cut off thy prey from the earth, and the voice of thy messengers shall no more be heard.
verses.bsb: “Behold, I am against you,” declares the LORD of Hosts. “I will send your chariots up in smoke, and the sword will devour your young lions. I will cut off your prey from the earth, and the voices of your messengers will no longer be heard.”

verses.esv: Woe to the bloody city, all full of lies and plunder— no end to the prey!
verses.kjv: WOE to the bloody city! it is all full of lies and robbery; the prey departeth not;
verses.bsb: Woe to the city of blood, full of lies, full of plunder, never without prey.

verses.esv: The crack of the whip, and rumble of the wheel, galloping horse and bounding chariot!
verses.kjv: The noise of a whip, and the noise of the rattling of the wheels, and of the pransing horses, and of the jumping chariots.
verses.bsb: The crack of the whip, the rumble of the wheel, galloping horse and bounding chariot!

verses.esv: Horsemen charging, flashing sword and glittering spear, hosts of slain, heaps of corpses, dead bodies without end— they stumble over the bodies!
verses.kjv: The horseman lifteth up both the bright sword and the glittering spear: and there is a multitude of slain, and a great number of carcases; and there is none end of their corpses; they stumble upon their corpses:
verses.bsb: Charging horseman, flashing sword, shining spear; heaps of slain, mounds of corpses, dead bodies without end— they stumble over their dead—

verses.esv: And all for the countless whorings of the prostitute, graceful and of deadly charms, who betrays nations with her whorings, and peoples with her charms.
verses.kjv: Because of the multitude of the whoredoms of the wellfavoured harlot, the mistress of witchcrafts, that selleth nations through her whoredoms, and families through her witchcrafts.
verses.bsb: because of the many harlotries of the harlot, the seductive mistress of sorcery, who betrays nations by her prostitution and clans by her witchcraft.

verses.esv: Behold, I am against you, declares the LORD of hosts, and will lift up your skirts over your face; and I will make nations look at your nakedness and kingdoms at your shame.
verses.kjv: Behold, I am against thee, saith the Lord of hosts; and I will discover thy skirts upon thy face, and I will shew the nations thy nakedness, and the kingdoms thy shame.
verses.bsb: “Behold, I am against you,” declares the LORD of Hosts. “I will lift your skirts over your face. I will show your nakedness to the nations and your shame to the kingdoms.

verses.esv: I will throw filth at you and treat you with contempt and make you a spectacle.
verses.kjv: And I will cast abominable filth upon thee, and make thee vile, and will set thee as a gazingstock.
verses.bsb: I will pelt you with filth and treat you with contempt; I will make a spectacle of you.

verses.esv: And all who look at you will shrink from you and say, “Wasted is Nineveh; who will grieve for her?” Where shall I seek comforters for you?
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that all they that look upon thee shall flee from thee, and say, Nineveh is laid waste: who will bemoan her? whence shall I seek comforters for thee?
verses.bsb: Then all who see you will recoil from you and say, ‘Nineveh is devastated; who will grieve for her?’ Where can I find comforters for you?”

verses.esv: Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile, with water around her, her rampart a sea, and water her wall?
verses.kjv: Art thou better than populous No, that was situate among the rivers, that had the waters round about it, whose rampart was the sea, and her wall was from the sea?
verses.bsb: Are you better than Thebes, stationed by the Nile with water around her, whose rampart was the sea, whose wall was the water?

verses.esv: Cush was her strength; Egypt too, and that without limit; Put and the Libyans were her helpers.
verses.kjv: Ethiopia and Egypt were her strength, and it was infinite; Put and Lubim were thy helpers.
verses.bsb: Cush and Egypt were her boundless strength; Put and Libya were her allies.

verses.esv: Yet she became an exile; she went into captivity; her infants were dashed in pieces at the head of every street; for her honored men lots were cast, and all her great men were bound in chains.
verses.kjv: Yet was she carried away, she went into captivity: her young children also were dashed in pieces at the top of all the streets: and they cast lots for her honourable men, and all her great men were bound in chains.
verses.bsb: Yet she became an exile; she went into captivity. Her infants were dashed to pieces at the head of every street. They cast lots for her dignitaries, and all her nobles were bound in chains.

verses.esv: You also will be drunken; you will go into hiding; you will seek a refuge from the enemy.
verses.kjv: Thou also shalt be drunken: thou shalt be hid, thou also shalt seek strength because of the enemy.
verses.bsb: You too will become drunk; you will go into hiding and seek refuge from the enemy.

verses.esv: All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs— if shaken they fall into the mouth of the eater.
verses.kjv: All thy strong holds shall be like fig trees with the firstripe figs: if they be shaken, they shall even fall into the mouth of the eater.
verses.bsb: All your fortresses are fig trees with the first ripe figs; when shaken, they fall into the mouth of the eater!

verses.esv: Behold, your troops are women in your midst. The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire has devoured your bars.
verses.kjv: Behold, thy people in the midst of thee are women: the gates of thy land shall be set wide open unto thine enemies: the fire shall devour thy bars.
verses.bsb: Look at your troops— they are like your women! The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire consumes their bars.

verses.esv: Draw water for the siege; strengthen your forts; go into the clay; tread the mortar; take hold of the brick mold!
verses.kjv: Draw thee waters for the siege, fortify thy strong holds: go into clay, and tread the morter, make strong the brickkiln.
verses.bsb: Draw your water for the siege; strengthen your fortresses. Work the clay and tread the mortar; repair the brick kiln!

verses.esv: There will the fire devour you; the sword will cut you off. It will devour you like the locust. Multiply yourselves like the locust; multiply like the grasshopper!
verses.kjv: There shall the fire devour thee; the sword shall cut thee off, it shall eat thee up like the cankerworm: make thyself many as the cankerworm, make thyself many as the locusts.
verses.bsb: There the fire will devour you; the sword will cut you down and consume you like a young locust. Make yourself many like the young locust; make yourself many like the swarming locust!

verses.esv: You increased your merchants more than the stars of the heavens. The locust spreads its wings and flies away.
verses.kjv: Thou hast multiplied thy merchants above the stars of heaven: the cankerworm spoileth, and flieth away.
verses.bsb: You have multiplied your merchants more than the stars of the sky. The young locust strips the land and flies away.

verses.esv: Your princes are like grasshoppers, your scribes like clouds of locusts settling on the fences in a day of cold— when the sun rises, they fly away; no one knows where they are.
verses.kjv: Thy crowned are as the locusts, and thy captains as the great grasshoppers, which camp in the hedges in the cold day, but when the sun ariseth they flee away, and their place is not known where they are.
verses.bsb: Your guards are like the swarming locust, and your scribes like clouds of locusts that settle on the walls on a cold day. When the sun rises, they fly away, and no one knows where.

verses.esv: Your shepherds are asleep, O king of Assyria; your nobles slumber. Your people are scattered on the mountains with none to gather them.
verses.kjv: Thy shepherds slumber, O king of Assyria: thy nobles shall dwell in the dust: thy people is scattered upon the mountains, and no man gathereth them.
verses.bsb: O king of Assyria, your shepherds slumber; your officers sleep. Your people are scattered on the mountains with no one to gather them.

verses.esv: There is no easing your hurt; your wound is grievous. All who hear the news about you clap their hands over you. For upon whom has not come your unceasing evil?
verses.kjv: There is no healing of thy bruise; thy wound is grievous: all that hear the bruit of thee shall clap the hands over thee: for upon whom hath not thy wickedness passed continually?
verses.bsb: There is no healing for your injury; your wound is severe. All who hear the news of you applaud your downfall, for who has not experienced your constant cruelty?
